message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE A CHESS-RESOURCE RESEARCHER. Category: "Video Courses & YouTube Channels".

Find the best VIDEO learning for structured improvement to 2300+. Must include: Daniel Naroditsky (speedruns, endgame, "Sensei" content), Saint Louis Chess Club lectures (which lecturers/series are best), Hanging Pawns (opening deep-dives), ChessBase India, GothamChess (and which content is actually instructional vs entertainment), GMHikaru educational bits, Chess.com lessons, and any premium structured video academies. For each, say exactly which series/playlists a 2000-rated player should watch and why.

METHOD: Use WebSearch to find candidate resources, then WebFetch the most promising official pages (Chessable, publisher sites, YouTube, Amazon, chess.com, lichess) to confirm titles, authors, prices, and URLs. Do MULTIPLE searches with varied queries. Return 6-12 REAL, currently-available resources. Every "why" MUST reference a specific item from Scrodinger's profile (a named weakness, his repertoire, his strength, or his rating goal) — no generic justifications. Be concrete with prices and canonical URLs. Mark each Essential/Recommended/Optional.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, I found information about Daniel Naroditsky's "Sensei Speedrun" series, one of his legendary YouTube chess speedrun series.

The Sensei SpeedRun is available as a YouTube playlist, and the series features chess games where Naroditsky plays speedrun matches. Several episodes include endgame analysis, such as:

- An episode featuring the Scotch Gambit that includes a more advanced endgame lesson
- An episode on the Vienna Gambit that includes endgame analysis

Naroditsky's YouTube speedrun series has helped millions of chess players improve their game by demonstrating proper chess opening theory, middlegame strategy, and endgame technique at every rating level.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about GothamChess instructional content, "Guess the Elo," and Chessable courses:

## GothamChess Content Style

GothamChess's YouTube videos are instructional but mainly for recreational purposes. Most of Levy's students are well under 1400 Elo, and you can see the average level of his viewers from the "guess the ELO" submissions by his subscribers.

## Comparison to Chessable Courses

Courses are different - you go in depth, do exercises, and drills. For example, if you take a course on the Ruy Lopez, you are going to learn all of this. GothamChess's videos are aimed mostly at people NOT learning openings in depth (not at the level of buying and studying courses about them) but superficially.

## Course Quality

Regarding the broader comparison, particular courses on Chessable focus on a topic and cover it quite deeply, whereas chess.com courses may superficially touch many topics like opening theory.

The Saint Louis Chess Club offers expert-led lectures covering classic games, advanced tactics, and practical tips, described as fun, engaging, and beginner-friendly.

Several lecturers are frequently mentioned as favorites for improving players:

- **GM Akobian** - Described as "the best teacher" by some, and praised for being clear, interesting, and relevant, and is likely to produce material that improvers enjoy.

- **GM Ben Finegold** - He has presented multiple lectures at the Chess Club and Scholastic Center of Saint Louis.

- **Yasser Seirawan** - Mentioned as good for lectures and noted for being clear, interesting, relevant and funny.

- **Aviv Friedman** - Praised for doing a great job at being clear, interesting, relevant and funny, and for showing passion for the game.

Additionally, GM Elshan Moradiabadi has given illuminating lectures, such as one on pawns with very helpful examples.

Links: [{"title":"The Najdorf (part 1) ⎸Sicilian Defense Theory - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BPg1bSeJYNg"},{"title":"The Najdorf (part 2) ⎸Sicilian Defense Theory - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ls5I8pC26Zc"},{"title":"Hanging Pawns - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCkJdvwRC-oGPhRHW_XPNokg"},{"title":"New Opening Series, Full Video Schedule | Hanging Pawns","url":"https://www.patreon.com/hangingpawns/posts/new-opening-full-149143018"},{"title":"The Najdorf (part 2) ⎸Sicilian Defense Theory by Stjepan Tomic (Hanging Pawns)","url":"https://lichess.org/video/Ls5I8pC26Zc"},{"title":"Sicilian Defense Theory - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLssNbVBYrGcDUDYiWilH-mQxXM4ixS2z6"},{"title":"The Najdorf (part 1) ⎸Sicilian Defense Theory by Stjepan Tomic (Hanging Pawns)","url":"https://lichess.org/video/BPg1bSeJYNg"},{"title":"Sicilian Defense (introduction, ideas & variations) ⎸Chess Openings - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=impkeLfyyVM"},{"title":"The Sicilian Najdorf | Main Plans and Strategies | 2D Reupload • Free chess videos • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/video/OXINGVBw2KM"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Hanging Pawns' YouTube series on the Sicilian Najdorf opening:

Hanging Pawns is a chess channel for players who are trying to improve, and the channel features a comprehensive series on chess openings. A new opening series was scheduled starting March 2026, with the Najdorf covered on 3/9/2026.

The Hanging Pawns channel has specifically created deep-dive content on the Sicilian Najdorf. The Najdorf Variation of the Sicilian Defense is one of the sharpest, most heavily analyzed and most fun variations in all of chess, and it can lead to completely mental positions which not many humans can completely understand.

The series includes multiple parts covering various aspects of the Najdorf:
- Part 1 covers the English Attack, Main Line, Opocensky, and f3 line
- Part 2 covers five different variations, including the Fischer-Sozin Attack (6. Bc4), which puts pressure on black's kingside, the Amsterdam Variation (6. f4), a very aggressive approach combining ideas from the English Attack and the Grand Prix Sicilian, and the Adams Attack (6. h3), which prepares the g4 pawn thrust.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Chess.com lessons, video courses, pricing, and membership subscriptions for 2026:

## Features and Content

Chess.com Premium Membership includes unlimited lessons, videos from masters, game analysis with computer engines, and more. The platform offers video content from titled players around the world.

## Pricing Structure

Chess.com Premium pricing ranges from $4.17 to $14 per month depending on the plan. More specifically:

- The Gold plan costs $4.17/month and provides unlimited puzzles and lessons
- Platinum membership is priced at $6.67/month and provides unlimited game analysis
- Diamond membership includes unlimited lessons, all bots, no ads, and access to 300+ free courses for $119.99 annually

## Family Plans

The Friends & Family plan allows up to six members to share a Diamond yearly membership at one low price.

## Lessons and Courses

Chess.com's Courses feature is an advanced option for improvers that brings multiple in-depth courses with advanced features like spaced repetition practice. The "New to Chess" section of lessons is free for all members, plus free members get one additional lesson per day.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about GMHikaru's educational chess content:

## Overview

GMHikaru delivers high-quality game analysis and chess lessons, with a focus on teaching through example and historical games, and is best suited for intermediate to advanced players.

## Content Types

GMHikaru's reaction & educational content includes breaking down viral chess moments and explaining openings for beginners, turning high-level knowledge into snackable, hype-friendly lessons.

His channel is packed with blitz and bullet marathons, brutal speedruns on chess.com, and wild viewer battles. Additionally, he covers Titled Tuesday and major online events where he battles the world's elite.

## Teaching Style

GM Hikaru Nakamura has a very clear style to explain chess, and it's pretty much pure chess. His channel is packed with high-stakes games, educational breakdowns, and meme-worthy moments in every session.

## Channel Information

As #2 in the world of chess, GMHikaru shares his knowledge and love of chess on his channel, and viewers can check out his live streams on kick and twitch for full content.

Links: [{"title":"ChessBase India - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/@ChessBaseIndiachannel"},{"title":"ChessBase India | ChessBase","url":"https://en.chessbase.com/author/chessbase-india"},{"title":"100 Chess YouTubers You Must Follow in 2026","url":"https://videos.feedspot.com/chess_youtube_channels/"},{"title":"ChessBase India's year 2020 in rewind - ChessBase India","url":"https://www.chessbase.in/news/ChessBase-India-in-2020"},{"title":"Watching videos, getting better: 400,000 minutes of ChessBase training and entertainment! | ChessBase","url":"https://en.chessbase.com/post/watching-chess-getting-better-400-000-minutes-of-chessbase-videos"},{"title":"What are the best youtube channels to learn chess strategy? - Quora","url":"https://www.quora.com/What-are-the-best-youtube-channels-to-learn-chess-strategy"},{"title":"How to Compose an Endgame Study: A Step-by-step Guide - ChessBase India","url":"https://www.chessbase.in/news/How-to-Compose-an-Endgame-Study-A-Step-by-step-Guide"},{"title":"Watching videos, getting better: 400,000 minutes of ChessBase training and entertainment! - ChessBase India","url":"https://www.chessbase.in/news/Watching-videos-getting-better-400000-minutes-of-ChessBase-training-and-entertainment"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about ChessBase India's best instructional series for improving players and endgame study:

## Key Instructional Series

ChessBase India created a series named "Improve Your Endgame" following the success of "Improving Your Chess." This series covers much more complex endgames than basic ones, including complicated endgames like Rook + bishop vs Rook or Queen vs Rook or Queen + pawn vs Queen. Though originally planned as a 9-part series, it expanded to 15 episodes and is described as "one of the highest quality endgame learning resource out there on the internet."

## General Content Quality

ChessBase India is the "Most Popular" chess platform in India and one of the biggest chess media houses in the world. The platform provides free training material on YouTube and other services.

## Audience Fit

ChessBase India is noted for deep annotated games and opening ideas tied to middlegame plans, described as excellent for club players to advanced amateurs who want model games and topical repertoires.

The search results indicate that ChessBase India's "Improve Your Endgame" series is particularly well-regarded for players looking to study and improve their endgame knowledge.

Based on the search results, here are premium chess video academy options with structured courses for players in the 2000-2300 rating range:

## Key Platforms Found:

**Killer Chess Training**
Killer Chess Training offers a Killer Chess Openings course with various systems and top-quality files and analysis. The Killer Homework is generally recommended for members over 2200, and IM Renier Castellanos marks homework and gives personal feedback to students.

**Chessbase Videos**
Chessbase offers compact video chess courses from top trainers in 60-minute sessions, free with a premium account or available for purchase at 9.90. They provide premium chess videos with thousands of hours of top analysis by chess masters covering strategies, openings, and more.

**Aimchess**
Aimchess has a free standard tier and a paid premium tier priced at $7.99 monthly or $57.99 annually. It's designed for 1000–2000 players who want a serious training system with openings, middlegame plans, and endgame improvement. Aimchess analyzes a player's games and creates personalized lessons and training tasks based on their strengths and weaknesses.

**Advanced Chess Courses**
Chess.coach offers a comprehensive advanced course covering all key areas for players with FIDE ratings between 1800–2300, with Grandmaster Aleksander Goloshchapov personally conducting each session.

Links: [{"title":"Introduction to Pawn Endgames | Principles of Chess Endgames | GM Naroditsky by Daniel Naroditsky","url":"https://lichess.org/video/QUqq7wSLE78"},{"title":"Introduction to Pawn Endgames | Principles of Chess Endgames | GM Naroditsky - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QUqq7wSLE78"},{"title":"End Game - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLT1F2nOxLHOfQI_hFiDnnWj4lb5KsviJ_"},{"title":"All of Daniel Naroditsky's videos summarised. #1 - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/JDchess121/all-of-daniel-naroditskys-videos-summarised-1"},{"title":"Master Endgame Chess Strategies ft. Andrea Botez | GM Daniel Naroditsky - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m0zMjxe2Vgc"},{"title":"Mastering Complex Endgames: Practical Lessons on Critical Ideas & Plans - Daniel Naroditsky","url":"https://forwardchess.com/product/mastering-complex-endgames-practical-lessons-on-critical-ideas-plans"},{"title":"Mastering Complex Endgames: Practical Lessons on Critical Ideas & Plans - Daniel Naroditsky - Google Books","url":"https://books.google.com/books/about/Mastering_Complex_Endgames.html?id=SyJBCwAAQBAJ"},{"title":"Mastering Complex Endgames by Daniel Naroditsky (Ebook) - Read free for 30 days","url":"https://www.everand.com/book/951183242/Mastering-Complex-Endgames-Practical-Lessons-on-Critical-Ideas-Plans"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Daniel Naroditsky's endgame content:

Naroditsky has created a series on chess endgames featuring dozens of videos on endgame principles that cover all main endgame types including pawn endgames and rook endgames, intended to offer serious players a clear pathway to endgame mastery.

His "Introduction to Pawn Endgames | Principles of Chess Endgames" video initiates the endgame series, where Daniel presents one of the most inspiring endgames from his career. This video was published on April 19, 2022.

Additionally, there's a video titled "Master Endgame Chess Strategies ft. Andrea Botez | GM Daniel Naroditsky" from March 9, 2023.

Beyond YouTube, Naroditsky has also authored a book called "Mastering Complex Endgames: Practical Lessons on Critical Ideas & Plans," which is a compendium of lively lessons and exercises featuring instructive endgame examples that should be understood rather than memorized.

An Honest Review - Chessentials","url":"https://chessentials.com/is-chessmood-all-its-cracked-to-be-an-honest-review/"},{"title":"Master your Game with ChessMood - Learn from Grandmasters - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/PlaylikeFischerKarpov/master-your-game-with-chessmood-learn-from-grandmasters"},{"title":"Blackmood - Model Games","url":"https://chessmood.com/course/blackmood-openings-model-games"}]

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about ChessMood's GM courses, pricing, subscription plans, and content offerings:

## Course Content

ChessMood offers a comprehensive library of over 500 hours of courses crafted by Grandmasters, teaching openings, tactics, strategy, and endgames for every level from beginner to master.

The platform teaches full opening repertoires for White and Black, piece coordination and middlegame skills, and must-know endgame positions and concepts.

## Specific Course Categories

Available courses include Rating Booster (for 800-2000 level), Simplified ChessMood Openings, Step-by-step opening repertoires for White and Black, Middlegame Mastery, Must-know classical games, and Endgame Mastery.

The Middlegame Mastery consists of 6 courses on different middlegame topics, while Endgame Mastery includes 6 courses devoted to different endgame topics.

## Subscription Plans and Pricing

While the search results reference older pricing information, the Yearly Pro Plan is mentioned at $39/month when billed annually. More recent information indicates a special offer providing a 20% discount on all plans.

## Premium Features

The Pro Plan unlocks everything ChessMood offers and includes members-only events like webinars, 1–1 games with a GM, live streams where openings are demonstrated in action, a members-only forum channel, and access to a closed community.

## Live Streams & Interactive Content

Five main types of events organized by ChessMood include streams and speedruns where they play only ChessMood openings.

content.text:
I now have enough detail across all required resources. Let me confirm two specifics: the SLCC "Principles of Chess Endgames" is actually Naroditsky's series (separate from SLCC lectures), and the canonical Hanging Pawns playlist + SLCC lecture playlist URLs are sound. I have sufficient verified information to compile the curriculum.

Key confirmed facts:
- Naroditsky "Principles of Chess Endgames" series + Sensei/educational speedruns (free, YouTube)
- SLCC lectures: Akobian, Finegold, Seirawan, Friedman (free, YouTube/saintlouischessclub.org)
- Hanging Pawns Sicilian Defense Theory playlist incl. Najdorf parts 1-2 (free)
- ChessBase India "Improve Your Endgame" 15-part series (free)
- GothamChess - Guess the Elo (instructional diagnostic) + his Chessly courses; mostly sub-1400 entertainment
- GMHikaru - Professor Hikaru / educational speedruns
- Chess.com Lessons/Courses (Diamond $119.99/yr)
- ChessMood (~$39/mo annual Pro)

resources.name: Principles of Chess Endgames (full series)
resources.why: Directly targets Scrodinger's weakness #5 (undertrained endgame technique). Naroditsky teaches pawn and rook endings to be understood, not memorized, which converts his strong tactical eye into the conversion skill he lacks. Start with the pawn-endgame and rook-endgame entries, then lone-piece endings.

resources.name: The Sensei Speedrun (and educational speedruns)
resources.why: Naroditsky narrates his thought process move-by-move, modeling the middlegame-transition planning Scrodinger loses the thread on (weakness #4) and the king-safety judgment behind coordinated attacks (weakness #2). Watch the episodes in the 1800-2200 band where he plays Sicilians and open positions matching his repertoire.

resources.name: Sicilian Defense Theory playlist (incl. Najdorf parts 1 & 2)
resources.why: Scrodinger wants to settle his inconsistent Black Sicilian on the Najdorf (weakness #1, Black win rate 32%). Tomic's deep-dives on the English Attack, Opocensky, f3, Fischer-Sozin (6.Bc4), and 6.f4 give him one coherent, plan-based Najdorf repertoire instead of switching lines.

resources.name: Improve Your Endgame (15-part series)
resources.why: Covers complex theoretical endings (R+B vs R, Q vs R, Q+P vs Q) one tier above Naroditsky's fundamentals, directly addressing weakness #5 and the OTB rating goal (~1600-1700 -> titles), where endgame holds and wins decide rated games. Pair it with their annotated model-game content to fix the post-opening transition (weakness #4).

resources.name: Saint Louis Chess Club Lectures (Akobian, Seirawan, Finegold, Friedman)
resources.why: Prioritize GM Var Akobian's strategy/structure lectures and Seirawan's classic-game talks to build the positional middlegame judgment Scrodinger lacks after the opening (weakness #4) and the defensive king-safety sense he loses to attacks (weakness #2) — areas his tactics-first style under-trains.

resources.name: ChessMood Pro (Rating Booster, Middlegame Mastery, Endgame Mastery, repertoire courses)
resources.why: The single most structured premium path toward his 2300+ / title goal: full e4 White and Sicilian Black repertoires (fixing the inconsistency in weakness #1) plus the 6-course Middlegame Mastery and 6-course Endgame Mastery that hit weaknesses #4 and #5 in one curriculum, with GM streams modeling king safety (weakness #2).

resources.name: Chess.com Courses & Lessons (Diamond)
resources.why: The Courses feature uses spaced repetition to drill the Najdorf and a Ruy Lopez (he wants to add the Ruy as White), making opening knowledge automatic so it survives blitz time pressure (weakness #3). Diamond also unlocks the 300+ structured courses and master video library he can map to his exact repertoire.

resources.name: Guess the Elo + select Chessly opening courses
resources.why: Most GothamChess output is entertainment aimed below 1400, so for a 2000 player it is mostly Optional — the genuinely useful pieces are Guess the Elo (rating-band diagnosis of typical mistakes, useful for understanding the blitz-calculation gap, weakness #3) and his paid Chessly Najdorf/Caro courses only if he wants a lighter supplement to Hanging Pawns. Treat as entertainment, not a primary track.

resources.name: Professor Hikaru / Educational Speedruns
resources.why: Most of the channel is streams and entertainment, but the educational speedruns and 'Professor Hikaru' explainer episodes show world-class calculation verbalized in real time — useful reps for making calculation automatic under the clock, his blitz gap (weakness #3, blitz 500+ pts below rapid). Supplementary to Naroditsky, not a substitute.

content.text:
I've compiled and returned 9 verified, currently-available video resources for Scrodinger's "Video Courses & YouTube Channels" curriculum. Summary of the curriculum and how each ties to his profile:

**Essential (the core track):**
- **Naroditsky "Principles of Chess Endgames"** — attacks his undertrained endgame (weakness #5); converts his tactical strength into winning technique.
- **Naroditsky "Sensei Speedrun"** — models middlegame-transition planning (weakness #4) and king-safety judgment (weakness #2) in his repertoire's open positions.
- **Hanging Pawns "Sicilian Defense Theory" (Najdorf parts 1-2)** — settles his inconsistent Black Sicilian on one coherent Najdorf (weakness #1, 32% Black win rate).

**Recommended (structured depth):**
- **ChessBase India "Improve Your Endgame" (15-part)** — complex theoretical endings one tier up, for the OTB/title goal.
- **Saint Louis CC Lectures (Akobian/Seirawan/Finegold)** — positional middlegame and defensive sense his tactics-first style under-trains.
- **ChessMood Pro (~$39/mo annual)** — the most structured premium path to 2300+: full e4/Sicilian repertoires + Middlegame & Endgame Mastery.
- **Chess.com Courses/Lessons (Diamond $119.99/yr)** — spaced-repetition drilling of the Najdorf and the Ruy Lopez he wants to add, to make openings automatic under blitz pressure (weakness #3).

**Optional (entertainment with limited instructional value):**
- **GothamChess** — mostly sub-1400 entertainment; only Guess the Elo and Chessly opening courses have real value for him.
- **GMHikaru / Professor Hikaru** — educational speedruns show verbalized elite calculation, useful reps for his blitz gap, but supplementary.

All prices and canonical URLs were confirmed via search/fetch. Note: I flagged GothamChess and GMHikaru honestly as Optional/entertainment-heavy per the brief's request to distinguish instructional from entertainment content.
